atten4.ptxt: By reading the DATA LIST displayed on an intelligent tester, you can check values, including those of the switches, sensors, and actuators, without removing any parts. Reading the DATA LIST as the first step in troubleshooting is one method of shortening diagnostic time.

atten3.ptxt: In the table below, the values listed under Normal Condition are reference only. Do not depend solely on these values when determining whether or not a part is faulty.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Enter the following menus: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / DATA LIST.
Check the values by referring to the table below.

entry.ptxt: Ignition timing advance for No. 1 cylinder:

item.ptxt: 10 to 20%: Idling
10 to 20%: Running without load at 2,500 rpm

entry.ptxt: Load percentage in terms of maximum intake air flow amount
Air flow rate from MAF meter:
8 to 19 g/sec.: Running without load at 2,500 rpm

item.ptxt: Mass air flow meter power source circuit open
VG circuit open or shorted

entry.ptxt: If value is 160.0 g/sec. or more:

item.ptxt: E2G circuit open
If value is -40°C (-40°F): sensor circuit open
If value is 140°C (284°F) or more: sensor circuit shorted
If value is -40°C (-40°F): sensor circuit open
If value is 140°C (284°F) or more: sensor circuit shorted

entry.ptxt: Ratio of evaporative purge flow to intake air volume:
EVAP system pressure monitored by canister pressure sensor
EVAP system pressure monitored by canister pressure sensor
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
2.6 to 4.5 V: Accelerator pedal fully depressed
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
3.4 to 5.0 V: Accelerator pedal fully depressed
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
Whether or not accelerator pedal position sensor detecting idle:
Accelerator fully closed learning value No. 1:
Accelerator fully closed learning value No. 2:
Whether or not fail safe function executed:
Whether or not fail safe function executed:

item.ptxt: 10 to 24%: Throttle fully closed
64 to 96%: Throttle fully open
Calculated value based on VTA1
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)

entry.ptxt: Whether or not throttle position sensor detecting idle:
Throttle position sensor No. 1 output voltage:
Throttle position sensor No. 2 output voltage:
Throttle position sensor No. 1 output voltage:

item.ptxt: 0.5 to 1.2 V: Throttle fully closed
3.2 to 4.8 V: Throttle fully open

entry.ptxt: Throttle position sensor No. 2 output voltage:

item.ptxt: 2.0 to 2.9 V: Throttle fully closed
4.6 to 5.0 V: Throttle fully open

entry.ptxt: Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
Throttle position sensor No. 1 output voltage (AD):
Whether or not throttle actuator control permitted:
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)

item.ptxt: When accelerator pedal is depressed, duty ratio increaseds
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)
When accelerator pedal is increaseds quickly, duty ratio increaseds
Read value with ignition switch ON (Do not start engine)

entry.ptxt: Heated oxygen sensor output voltage for bank 1 sensor 2:
0.1 to 0.9 V: Driving at 70 km/h (44 mph)
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
Heated oxygen sensor output voltage for bank 2 sensor 2:
0.1 to 0.9 V: Driving at 70 km/h (44 mph)
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
A/F sensor output voltage for bank 1 sensor 1:
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
A/F sensor output voltage for bank 2 sensor 1:
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
Average value for fuel system of bank 1:
Average value for fuel system of bank 2:
Short-term fuel compensation used to maintain air-fuel ratio at stoichiometric air-fuel ratio
Short-term fuel compensation used to maintain air-fuel ratio at stoichiometric air-fuel ratio
Overall fuel compensation carried out in long-term to compensate for continual deviation of short-term fuel trim from central value
Overall fuel compensation carried out in long-term to compensate for continual deviation of short-term fuel trim from central value
Fuel system status (Bank 1): OL or CL or OL DRIVE or OL FAULT or CL FAULT

item.ptxt: OL (Open Loop): Has not yet satisfied conditions to go closed loop
CL (Closed Loop): Using A/F sensor as feedback for fuel control
OL DRIVE: Open loop due to driving conditions (fuel enrichment)
OL FAULT: Open loop due to detected system fault
CL FAULT: Closed loop but A/F sensor, which used for fuel control malfunctioning

entry.ptxt: Fuel system status (Bank 2): OL or CL or OL DRIVE or OL FAULT or CL FAULT

item.ptxt: OL (Open Loop): Has not yet satisfied conditions to go closed loop
CL (Closed Loop): Using A/F sensor as feedback for fuel control
OL DRIVE: Open loop due to driving conditions (fuel enrichment)
OL FAULT: Open loop due to detected system fault
CL FAULT: Closed loop but A/F sensor, which used for fuel control, malfunctioning

entry.ptxt: Short-term fuel trim associated with bank 1 sensor 2:
Short-term fuel trim associated with bank 2 sensor 2:
Short-term fuel trim associated with bank 1 sensor 1:

item.ptxt: Value less than 1 (0.000 to 0.999) = Lean
Stoichiometric air-fuel ratio = 1
Value greater than 1 (1.001 to 1.999) = Rich

entry.ptxt: Short-term fuel trim associated with bank 2 sensor 1:

item.ptxt: Value less than 1 (0.000 to 0.999) = Lean
Stoichiometric air-fuel ratio = 1
Value greater than 1 (1.001 to 1.999) = Rich

entry.ptxt: Sub heated oxygen sensor impedance (bank 1 sensor 2):
Sub heated oxygen sensor impedance (bank 2 sensor 2):
Engine coolant temperature at engine start:
Intake air temperature at engine start:
Starter switch signal: ON or OFF
ON: When steering wheel is first turned after battery terminals connected
Signal status usually ON until battery terminals disconnected

item.ptxt: ON: Throttle fully closed
OFF: Throttle open

entry.ptxt: ON: Headlights or defogger turned ON
Whether or not electric throttle control system power input:
VSV for Acoustic Control Induction System Status:
Key-off EVAP system leak detection pump status:
Key-off EVAP system vent valve status:
VSV for Air Intake Control System Status:
TC and CG (TE1) terminal of DLC3:
Engine Speed of Cylinder #1 of Injection Stopped
Engine Speed of Cylinder #21 of Injection Stopped
Engine Speed of Cylinder #3 of Injection Stopped
Engine Speed of Cylinder #4 of Injection Stopped
Engine Speed of Cylinder #5 of Injection Stopped
Engine Speed of Cylinder #6 of Injection Stopped
Avg Eng Spd, All Cylinder of Injection Stopped
VVT OCV operation duty (bank 1 Intake side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
VVT exhaust hold duty ratio learning value (bank 1 Exhaust side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
VVT OCV operation duty (bank 1 Exhaust side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
VVT OCV operation duty (bank 2 Intake side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
VVT exhaust hold duty ratio learning value (bank 2 Exhaust side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
VVT exhaust operation duty (bank 2 Exhaust side):
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
FC IDL = "ON" when throttle valve fully closed and engine speed over 2,800 rpm
Fuel cut TAU (Fuel cut with very light load):
Fuel cut being performed with very light load to prevent incomplete engine combustion
This item displayed only during idling
This item displayed only during idling
This item displayed only during idling
This item displayed only during idling
This item displayed only during idling
This item displayed only during idling
Engine RPM for first misfire range:
Engine load for first misfire range:
Check mode result for vehicle speed sensor:
Check mode result for misfire monitor:
Check mode result for HO2 sensor (bank 2):
Check mode result for HO2 sensor (bank 1):
Check mode result for air-fuel ratio sensor (bank 1):
Check mode result for air-fuel ratio sensor (bank 2):
Number of warm-up cycles after DTC cleared

item.ptxt: Normal Condition: If no idling conditions are specified, the transmission gear selector lever should be in the N or P position, and the A/C switch and all accessory switches should be OFF.
*: DATA LIST values are displayed only when performing the following ACTIVE TESTs: VVT B1, VVT B2, VVT EX B1 and VVT EX B2. For other ACTIVE TESTs, the DATA LIST value will be 0.

atten4.ptxt: Performing an ACTIVE TEST enables components including the relays, VSV (Vacuum Switching Valve) and actuators, to be operated without removing any parts. The ACTIVE TEST can be performed with an intelligent tester. Performing an ACTIVE TEST as the first step in troubleshooting is one method of shortening diagnostic time.

item.ptxt: DATA LIST can be displayed during ACTIVE TEST.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Enter the following menus: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / ACTIVE TEST.
Perform the ACTIVE TEST by referring to the table below.

item.ptxt: All injectors tested at the same time
Perform test at less than 3,000 rpm
Injection volume can be changed in 1 % graduations within control range
Perform test at less than 3,000 rpm
A/F CONTROL enables to check and to graph A/F (Air Fuel Ratio) sensor and Heated Oxygen (HO2) sensor voltage outputs
To conduct test, select following menu items: ACTIVE TEST / A/F CONTROL / USER DATA / AFS B1S1 and O2S B1S2, and press YES and ENTER followed by F4
ON: ACIS VSV ON
OFF: ACIS VSV OFF

entry.ptxt: Turn on and off TC and TE1 connection

item.ptxt: ON: TC and TE1 connected
OFF: TC and TE1 disconnected

entry.ptxt: Test is possible when following conditions are met:

item.ptxt: Ignition switch is on (IG)
Ignition is stopped

entry.ptxt: Test is possible when following conditions are met:

item.ptxt: Ignition switch is on (IG)
Ignition is stopped

entry.ptxt: Test is possible when following conditions are met:

item.ptxt: Ignition switch is on (IG)
Ignition is stopped
Accelerator pedal is fully depressed (APP: 58 degrees or more)

entry.ptxt: This valve added to present OCV control duty
Engine stalls or roughly idles when VVT actuator is operated by 100 %.

item.ptxt: Engine stalls or idles roughly when OCV turned ON
Normal engine running or idling when OCV OFF

entry.ptxt: Engine stalls or roughly idles when VVT actuator is operated by 100 %.

item.ptxt: Engine stalls or idles roughly when OCV turned ON
Normal engine running or idling when OCV OFF

entry.ptxt: This valve added to present OCV control duty
Test is possible during vehicle stopping and engine idling.
Control the all cylinder fuel cut
Fuel injection and ignition stop in all cylinders.

atten4.ptxt: Performing a SYSTEM CHECK enables the system, which consists of multiple actuators, to be operated without removing any parts. In addition, it can show whether or not any DTCs are set, and can detect potential malfunctions in the system. The SYSTEM CHECK can be performed with an intelligent tester.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Enter the following menus: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / SYSTEM CHECK.
Perform the SYSTEM CHECK by referring to the table below.

entry.ptxt: Long: Indicates fuel consumption for maximum 60 sec.
Short: Indicates fuel consumption for maximum 20 sec.
While driving (vehicle speed 3.1 mph (5 km/h) or more)
Perform 5 steps in order to operate EVAP key-off monitor automatically
Perform 5 steps in order to operate EVAP key-off monitor manually
